user_message: The CSA escalated Humongous Insurance to me -- can you create the summary?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 15:55 EST
Subject: Re: Re: consumption trend Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with consumption trend. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 09:27 EST
Subject: Re: Re: consumption trend Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with consumption trend. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 11:04 EST
Subject: Re: Re: consumption trend Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Humongous Insurance
Date: 2024-01-12
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance), David Chen (Microsoft), Michelle (Humongous Insurance)

[00:02:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:05:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:10:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:12:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Humongous Insurance: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
